prompt: Develop a MedOps Offline Operations Console system for community clinics and pharmacy-adjacent outpatient centers to manage regulated inventory, staff learning, memberships, and facilities work orders in one offline desktop workspace. Primary roles include System Administrator (local security, configuration, and backups), Inventory Pharmacist/Materials Manager (drug/consumable receiving, dispensing deductions, and counts), Learning Coordinator (content curation and competency review), Front Desk (membership validation and benefit redemption), and Maintenance Supervisor/Technician (repair intake and closure). The desktop UI must run on Windows 11 with a baseline of 1920x1080 and high-DPI scaling, support multi-window parallel work (e.g., inventory receiving open alongside a work-order queue), right-click context menus on tables (quick adjust, void, print, export), and keyboard-first shortcuts (Ctrl+K global search, Ctrl+N new record, Ctrl+Enter save, F2 edit row). A system tray mode must allow “lock screen” without quitting, show local reminders (membership expiration in 14/7/1 days; low-stock alerts), and provide one-click backup. Learning content is managed in a subject → chapter → knowledge-point hierarchy with tags and multi-dimensional classifications; staff can full-text search by title/keyword/tag and import/export Markdown/HTML or local files. Repair requests are submitted by staff as tenants of the facility with photos, description, location text, and priority; the system auto-dispatches based on trade and workload, enforces SLA targets (Urgent 4 hours, High 24 hours, Normal 3 business days), captures parts/labor cost, assigns responsibility, collects a 1–5 rating at close, and archives for trend analytics. Memberships support tiers, points, stored-value and session packages, freeze/unfreeze, and strict benefit validation (no redemption after expiration; partial session redemption not allowed; stored-value supports refunds only within 7 days if unused). The application is built as a desktop shell (Electron-based UI with React) embedding a local Go service using Echo, with all data persisted to a local PostgreSQL instance bundled with the installer (.msi). No internet dependencies are permitted: authentication is local username/password with minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, and role-based access control; sensitive fields (real-name verification status, deposits, and violation notes) are encrypted at rest using a locally stored key protected by OS credential storage, and masked on-screen by default. Core data models include SKU with NDC/UPC, batch/lot, expiration date, storage location, and unit-of-measure; stock in/out transactions require reason codes, and prescription-based deduction enforces “cannot dispense past expiration” and “cannot reduce below zero,” with low-stock thresholds per SKU and a monthly stocktake workflow producing variance and loss records. Charges and settlement support offline rate tables (distance in miles entered manually or imported via USB CSV, weight/volume tiering, fuel surcharge %, and taxable flags), generate statements by date range, reconcile variances with mandatory notes for deltas over $25.00, route settlements through two-step approvals, and export signed CSV/JSON files for downstream systems. Local file operations must deduplicate attachments by SHA-256 fingerprint, store them in an app-managed directory with retention rules, and allow export of audit-ready ZIP bundles. The system must cold-start in under 10 seconds on standard office PCs, remain stable for 30-day uptime with explicit resource disposal, implement automatic state recovery (draft checkpoints every 30 seconds for forms), and support offline updates via imported packages with a one-click rollback to the previous version.
